questions.q: Of which deity is Krishna traditionally regarded as the eighth avatar?
questions.explanation: Krishna is honoured in the Puranas as the eighth avatar of Vishnu, the preserver.

questions.q: In which city was Krishna born, according to the Bhagavata Purana?
questions.explanation: The Bhagavata Purana recounts that Krishna was born in Mathura, in the prison of King Kamsa.

questions.q: Who is the birth-mother of Krishna in the texts?
questions.explanation: Krishna's birth-mother is Devaki, wife of Vasudeva, though he was raised by the cowherd Yashoda.

questions.q: Which musical instrument is most closely associated with Krishna in his pastoral lila?
questions.explanation: Krishna is famed for enchanting the gopis and cattle with the music of his flute (bansuri).

questions.q: In which forest region did Krishna spend his celebrated childhood among the cowherds?
questions.explanation: Krishna's childhood lila unfolded in Gokula and Vrindavan, by the banks of the Yamuna.

questions.q: Which tyrannical king, Krishna's uncle, did Krishna slay according to the Bhagavata Purana?
questions.explanation: The Bhagavata Purana describes Krishna slaying the tyrant Kamsa, the cruel king of Mathura.

questions.q: Which mountain did Krishna lift to shelter the people from a great storm?
questions.explanation: Krishna lifted Mount Govardhan on his finger to shelter Vrindavan from Indra's downpour.

questions.q: Who was the foster-mother who lovingly raised Krishna in Gokula?
questions.explanation: Krishna was raised among cowherds by his foster-mother Yashoda and her husband Nanda.

questions.q: The young women of Vrindavan devoted to Krishna are known by which name?
questions.explanation: The gopis, the cowherd maidens of Vrindavan, were devoted companions in Krishna's lila.

questions.q: Krishna is often depicted with skin of which colour?
questions.explanation: Krishna is traditionally portrayed with dark blue or blue-black skin, a hallmark of his iconography.

questions.q: Who was the father of Krishna, imprisoned by Kamsa along with Devaki?
questions.explanation: Vasudeva, husband of Devaki, was Krishna's father and carried the infant to safety in Gokula.

questions.q: Which elder brother of Krishna is associated with the plough and is counted among Vishnu's incarnations?
questions.explanation: Balarama, Krishna's elder brother who bears the plough, is regarded as an incarnation of the serpent Shesha.

questions.q: Which multi-hooded serpent did the young Krishna subdue in the river Yamuna?
questions.explanation: Krishna danced upon and subdued the venomous serpent Kaliya, cleansing the waters of the Yamuna.

questions.q: On which sacred scripture is Krishna's discourse to Arjuna recorded?
questions.explanation: Krishna's teaching to Arjuna on the battlefield is preserved as the Bhagavad Gita within the Mahabharata.

questions.q: In the Mahabharata, for which warrior did Krishna serve as charioteer at Kurukshetra?
questions.explanation: Krishna served as the charioteer and counsellor of Arjuna during the great war at Kurukshetra.

questions.q: What is the name of the discus weapon wielded by Krishna?
questions.explanation: Krishna wields the Sudarshana chakra, the spinning discus of Vishnu, as his foremost weapon.

questions.q: What is the name of Krishna's conch, sounded at the start of the Kurukshetra war?
questions.explanation: Krishna's conch is named Panchajanya, sounded upon the field of Kurukshetra in the Mahabharata.

questions.q: To which coastal city did Krishna lead his people, establishing his kingdom in later life?
questions.explanation: Krishna founded the kingdom of Dwaraka on the western coast after departing Mathura.

questions.q: Which deity's worship did Krishna challenge when he urged the people to honour Mount Govardhan instead?
questions.explanation: Krishna redirected the people's worship from Indra to Mount Govardhan, prompting Indra's storm.

questions.q: Which queen is named in the Bhagavata Purana as the principal consort of Krishna at Dwaraka?
questions.explanation: Rukmini is celebrated in the Bhagavata Purana as the chief queen and consort of Krishna at Dwaraka.

questions.q: Which gopi of Vrindavan is most cherished in devotional tradition as Krishna's beloved?
questions.explanation: Radha is revered in devotional tradition as the foremost beloved gopi of Krishna in Vrindavan.

questions.q: Which Purana is the principal source for the life and lila of Krishna?
questions.explanation: The Bhagavata Purana is the principal text recounting Krishna's birth, childhood lila, and deeds.

questions.q: What is the name of the demoness who tried to kill the infant Krishna with poisoned milk?
questions.explanation: The Bhagavata Purana relates that the demoness Putana sought to kill the infant Krishna with her poisoned breast.

questions.q: In the Bhagavata Purana, which musical assembly describes Krishna's nocturnal dance with the gopis?
questions.explanation: The Rasa lila is the celebrated circular dance of Krishna with the gopis described in the Bhagavata Purana.

questions.q: Which powerful king of Magadha, father-in-law of Kamsa, repeatedly besieged Krishna's Mathura?
questions.explanation: Jarasandha, king of Magadha and father-in-law of Kamsa, repeatedly attacked Mathura to avenge Kamsa.

questions.q: What is the name of Krishna's poor childhood friend whose offering of beaten rice he honoured?
questions.explanation: The Bhagavata Purana tells how Krishna lovingly received the humble gift of beaten rice from his friend Sudama.

questions.q: Which sister of Krishna became the wife of the Pandava hero Arjuna?
questions.explanation: Subhadra, the sister of Krishna and Balarama, married Arjuna and was the mother of Abhimanyu.

questions.q: At the court of the Kauravas, whose garment did Krishna miraculously extend to preserve her honour?
questions.explanation: The Mahabharata recounts that Krishna miraculously extended Draupadi's garment to protect her in the Kaurava court.

questions.q: Which vain king of Chedi did Krishna slay with the Sudarshana chakra at Yudhishthira's sacrifice?
questions.explanation: Krishna slew Shishupala, king of Chedi, with the Sudarshana chakra after his hundred offences at the Rajasuya sacrifice.

questions.q: In the Bhagavata Purana, by what means does Krishna's earthly life come to its close?
options: A hunter's arrow striking his foot
questions.explanation: The Bhagavata Purana relates that a hunter named Jara, mistaking Krishna's foot for a deer, struck it with an arrow, ending his earthly sojourn.
